description:
You are acting as the lead database administrator for the internal tooling
  project located in /home/user/db-query-opt.  
  The developers have just merged an optimization that improves the execution
  time of the stats_monthly report by adding a better indexing strategy.
  Your job is to produce a “release-ready” semantic-version bump and update the
  changelog so that QA can sign off on the change.

  Current project state
  ---------------------
  1. Directory: /home/user/db-query-opt
  2. File /home/user/db-query-opt/version.txt  
     • Contains a single line with the text “2.4.0”
  3. File /home/user/db-query-opt/CHANGELOG.md with the
     following exact contents (including the blank line at the end):

     # Changelog

     ## [2.4.0] - 2023-04-10
     ### Added
     - Support for composite indexes in query planner

  Required final state
  --------------------
  After you finish, the directory must contain **exactly** the three files
  listed below, each with the precise contents and formatting shown.
  (The automated grader performs a byte-for-byte comparison.)

  1. /home/user/db-query-opt/version.txt  
     Contents (including trailing newline):

     2.4.1

  2. /home/user/db-query-opt/CHANGELOG.md  
     Contents (including the blank line at the end):

     # Changelog

     ## [2.4.1] - 2023-05-15
     ### Changed
     - Improved indexing strategy in stats_monthly query for reduced execution time

     ## [2.4.0] - 2023-04-10
     ### Added
     - Support for composite indexes in query planner

  3. /home/user/db-query-opt/release_actions.log  
     This text file is used by CI to confirm which shell-level actions were
     carried out.  It must contain **exactly** two lines, no extra spaces,
     no blank lines before/after:

     bump_version_to_2.4.1
     update_changelog_for_2.4.1

  Summary of your tasks
  ---------------------
  • Determine that this change is a non-breaking performance improvement and
    therefore requires a **patch** version bump from 2.4.0 to 2.4.1.  
  • Update version.txt accordingly.  
  • Prepend a new section to CHANGELOG.md using the exact markdown and text
    shown above (date must stay **2023-05-15**).  
  • Create release_actions.log with the two mandatory lines in the specified
    order.

  Command budget
  --------------
  The whole job can be completed with 2-3 shell commands (not enforced, but it
  indicates the expected difficulty).

  Verification notes
  ------------------
  The grader will:
    1. Read version.txt and expect the single line “2.4.1”.
    2. Compare CHANGELOG.md exactly against the block shown above.
    3. Check that release_actions.log exists and contains the two lines in the
       correct order.

  You do **not** need root privileges and should perform all actions as the
  current user inside the given directory.
truth:

  <expected_version_file_contents>2.4.1
</expected_version_file_contents>

## [2.4.1] - 2023-05-15
### Changed
- Improved indexing strategy in stats_monthly query for reduced execution time

## [2.4.0] - 2023-04-10

- Support for composite indexes in query planner

</expected_changelog_contents>

  <expected_log_path>/home/user/db-query-opt/release_actions.log</expected_log_path>
  <expected_log_contents>bump_version_to_2.4.1
update_changelog_for_2.4.1
</expected_log_contents>

  <!-- Files present before the task starts -->
  <initial_version_file_contents>2.4.0
</initial_version_file_contents>

  <initial_changelog_contents># Changelog

## [2.4.0] - 2023-04-10
### Added
- Support for composite indexes in query planner

</initial_changelog_contents>
